item.title: Young and Under 30 Project - Review
item.content:encoded:
I found this article through psul's 'Young and Under 30' project to draw more attention and feedback to articles that seem to have missed it the first time round. So follows my review.

+ What works
- Hide

This is a classic spooky story - a phone that lets you hear how people (might) die. The ambiguity of what is going on here, and whether these are supposed to be read as preductions, is quite nice, and I actually really like the flavour regarding the Swedish woman and the church at the other end. The final log is pretty good at following a balancing act of dealing with cryptic entities - the woman seems ethereal, but I also got the impression she's going along with the conversation because she's bored and wants someone to talk to.

+ What could be improved
- Hide 

The concept might be a little barebones, but I'd wager this problem is exaggerated by the execution, which stretches the article out and limits the time available to get into the interesting material (the ghost, the church in Sweden). There's a few conceptual things I'm not too fond of: -3's sudden appearance seems like a transparent plot device to end the interview and up the stakes in ways that the article isn't interested in following through on. The depression effect seems tacked on and doesn't add much, and the 'can the Foundation make predictions with it' angle just doesn't go anywhere.

The biggest problem I have here is the basic writing style, which isn't very fluid and becomes slightly incoherent rather frequently. There are a lot of odd lines, like describing how the phone is 'completely functional' but all attempts to actually use it normally 'have concluded with failure' - that's a self-contradicting line, but 'have concluded with failure' is also really wordy and clunky when 'fail' would have done the same thing. There's a bunch of odd, clunky lines like this - the compulsion 'has been noted possible to overcome', 'An SCP-3239-2 instance will transmit both voice and environmental audio correlating to specific causes to the instance's death', 'Questioning… resulted in a coinciding reference to a "haunted telephone," etc. It's most prevalent in the description of SCP-3239-2, and in the first log batch.

The first log batch is probably the weakest thing in the article - it's long, and the decision to write it all as an after-test report makes it a bit of a chore to read, especially given the style issues and the fact that, ultimately, we don't learn all that much. It just adds padding before we get to the last log, where we start learning things. I'd either cut the first log entirely, or reformat it as an audio log and trim to just to important, consequential information.

+ How to improve it
- Hide

To improve the article, I'd recommend re-angling this to have more of an emphasis on the church and the ghost (they're the most striking things in here), and cut back on the extraneous effects. The church situation is underdeveloped (and the __34 blackbox is awful expungement - you should just tell us the date, it's not going to ruin the article). Stuff like the depression angle could probably be safely cut.

I would cut the first set of logs entirely or replace them with audio logs which get to the punch quickly for readability. And I would absolutely go back and rewrite for clarity and fluency.

Final Verdict: Downvote, though I'd be optimistic if this was redrafted. It's a classic spooky ghost story, which might not be the most original thing on the planet, but there's enough here for me to see some real potential. As it stands, though, it's too loose, going in too many directions and not focusing enough on the intriguing stuff. It could really use some pruning of the repetitive, extraneous logs and use that wordcount to flesh out the final interview and addendum some more. At absolute minimum though, this badly needs a rewrite for fluency, because the stylistic mistakes are just weighing the whole endeavour down at the moment.

+ More about YaU30
- Hide

The Young and under 30 project aims to bring attention to articles that are less than a year old, and have a score of less than +30. These are often the articles getting the least attention on site, and we want to change that!

Here's how you can help: read this article, vote, and comment on why you voted the way you did. Your comments don't have to be in this format - write it however you like. And if you use IRC, give this post a plug on the chat to get more commenters over here - you can find the direct link by clicking "Options" —> "Permanent link".

You don't have to wait for posts like this one - you can initiate discussion yourself on other Yau30 articles. A list of eligible articles can be found here. Remember to update the list to track any articles that you've reviewed.

item.title: First SCP. Yay.
item.content:encoded:
Title explains it, really. Huge thanks to these people for the feedback on during both the brainstorming and drafting (I don't know how to do the profile link thing, so I'll just do an old fashioned link):
Golden506
Veiedhimaedhr
Zyn
Ma_Kir

Phone photo authored by Holger Ellgaard under the Creative Commons Attribution-Sharealike 3.0 Unported license. Here's link to original: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ericsson_bakelittelefon_1931.jpg

Church photo authored by Bernt Fransson underthe Creative Commons Attribution-Sharealike 3.0 Unported license. Here's link to original: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ellinghem02.jpg
